page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine instance within the ecs.ic5 server family, running on the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor operating at a baseline frequency of 2.5 GHz. The system is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 1.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include any local NVMe SSD storage or GPU hardware accelerators, and it provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that the ecs.ic5.xlarge operates in the lower performance tiers. The Intel Xeon 8163 processor achieved a single-core stress-ng score of 1102.69 and a multi-core score of 2935.19, both falling into the bottom 25% of tested servers. Memory performance is constrained, with a read-only large block bandwidth of 20.87 GB/sec and a PassMark memory score of 1229.28, placing it in the bottom 10%. Computational tasks such as software compilation with Clang scored 2919.0, while single-threaded gzip compression registered at 19.26 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the ecs.ic5.xlarge offers dedicated CPU resources at a lower entry point, but it presents significant tradeoffs due to its highly restricted memory capacity of 1.0 GB per core and the absence of local storage. The low memory bandwidth and weak overall CPU benchmarks limit its utility for resource-intensive applications. This instance is best suited for lightweight, compute-bound workloads that do not require substantial memory footprints, such as basic static web serving, minor text processing, or low-demand utility scripting.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.xlarge is an x86_64 virtual server in the ecs.ic5 family, utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores running at 2.5 GHz and 4.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a low 1.0 GB of RAM per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks place this server in the lower tiers, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 2935.19 and a PassMark memory score in the bottom 10%. Due to these memory and processing constraints, the ecs.ic5.xlarge is best suited for lightweight, non-memory-intensive workloads such as basic static web serving, simple text processing, and low-demand utility scripts where dedicated CPU allocation is required without high memory overhead.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.xlarge is a KVM-based virtual server featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 cores, 4.0 GB of RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for lightweight, low-memory compute workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.ic5.xlarge is a KVM virtual server with 4 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 4 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps bandwidth for lightweight compute tasks.
tagline: A dedicated four-core virtual server with entry-level memory capacity designed for lightweight, compute-bound utility workloads and basic web serving.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 physical cores at 2.5 GHz
Configured with 4.0 GB of system memory (1.0 GB per core)
Baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps with no local storage
Best suited for lightweight static web serving and basic text processing
Delivers dedicated CPU allocation for entry-level compute tasks
